name: 14q12_FOXG1_duplication
description: 14q12 microduplication / microdeletion encompassing FOXG1. Deletions and intragenic LoF cause the congenital variant of Rett syndrome (FOXG1 syndrome); duplications cause infantile spasms / West syndrome and severe ID. Breakpoints typically non-recurrent but a subset is mediated by flanking SD blocks at 14q12.

architecture.internal_structure: Flanking SD blocks of ~10-50 kb mediate a fraction of recurrent breakpoints; unique core contains FOXG1 (primary dosage gene), NOVA1, PRKD1. Most cases involve focal FOXG1 CNV or intragenic mutation.
